content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Los Angeles Airport (LAX) Lost\
  \ Property | Report a Lost Item
- url: https://lost-found.org/airport-los-angeles-international-lax/
\
  - source: Lost-Found.org
- domain: lost-found.org

## Content

# Lost and Found\
  \ — Los Angeles International Airport

This page provides general information\
  \ about lost property at Los Angeles International Airport and offers a Lost & Found\
  \ Reporting Form hosted on the platform.

The platform advises travelers to report\
  \ a missing item promptly and to provide detailed information, including:

- the\
  \ terminal;
- the gate, restaurant, shop, checkpoint, or other location;
- the\
  \ date and approximate time;
- the flight information when relevant;
- a detailed\
  \ description of the item.

The page states that users may submit its reporting\
  \ form so that the platform's team can assist with locating lost property.

Commonly\
  \ reported items include phones, laptops, headphones, wallets, passports, travel\
  \ documents, glasses, clothing, chargers, bags, cameras, jewelry, keys, and medical\
  \ items.

## Location Guidance

The page encourages travelers to record the\
  \ terminal and exact location because LAX has multiple terminals and many separate\
  \ operational areas.

It provides reminders for several locations:

- At a checkpoint,\
  \ check screening bins and the surrounding area.
- At a gate, check seats, seat\
  \ pockets, charging areas, and the boarding area.
- In restaurants, shops, restrooms,\
  \ and waiting areas, check counters, tables, hooks, and seating.
- For international\
  \ travel, consider customs, immigration, currency-exchange, and duty-free areas.
\
  
## Airport and Aircraft Processes

The page lists contact information and operating-hour\
  \ information for a Los Angeles airport lost-property office.

It also states\
  \ that items left onboard an aircraft should be handled by the airline. Airlines\
  \ maintain separate procedures for property found on their aircraft.

If an item\
  \ is found through the airport process, collection or shipping arrangements may\
  \ be available. Shipping may involve a charge.

The page presents a Lost & Found\
  \ Reporting Form hosted by its own platform alongside general airport and airline-routing\
  \ information.
"

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Damaged, Delayed or Lost Baggage | Delta Air Lines

  - url: https://www.delta.com/us/en/baggage/delayed-lost-damaged-baggage

  - source: Delta Air Lines

  - domain: delta.com


  ## Content


  # Lost Personal Items on a Delta Aircraft or at the Airport


  Delta separates personal items left during travel from checked-baggage problems.


  A personal item left on a Delta aircraft, at a Delta Sky Club, at an airport counter,
  or in a gate area should be reported through Delta''s Lost and Found process. Delta
  states that it will attempt to locate and return the item.


  To report a personal item:


  1. Open the Delta Lost and Found page.

  2. Select the option for items left onboard an aircraft, in a gate area, or in a
  Delta Sky Club.

  3. Open the official report form at `https://app.nettracer.aero/lf-pax/delta/report`.

  4. Complete the lost-item form.

  5. Submit the report.


  The report link opens Delta''s lost-item reporting service on the `app.nettracer.aero`
  domain. Travelers who have already submitted a lost-item report should use the existing-report
  or Lost and Found FAQ options to review status or obtain help with that report.


  ## Distinguishing a Personal Lost Item from Baggage Claims


  The personal lost-item process is different from the procedures for delayed, damaged,
  or lost checked bags.


  For delayed checked baggage, Delta instructs travelers to begin with a report through
  a Delta Baggage Service Office and retain the file reference number. Claims involving
  a checked bag, missing contents from a checked bag, damage, or expense reimbursement
  follow separate baggage-claim procedures and deadlines.


  A wallet, passport holder, phone, laptop, or similar personal article left in an
  aircraft cabin or a Delta-controlled airport location should not be treated as a
  delayed checked-bag claim merely because it was lost during a flight.


  ## Information Needed for a Useful Report


  A detailed report improves the ability to match an item. Relevant information may
  include:


  - the flight and travel date;

  - the airport and location where the item may have been left;

  - the seat, gate, counter, or club location when known;

  - a detailed description of the item;

  - distinctive features that can help verify ownership;

  - current contact information.


  The personal lost-item form is the route identified by Delta for items associated
  with Delta aircraft and specified Delta-controlled airport locations. The page does
  not state that every item missing somewhere in an airport belongs to Delta. The
  location and current custody of the item remain important when selecting the responsible
  lost-property process.

  '

title: Damaged, Delayed or Lost Baggage | Delta Air Lines

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: LAX Lost & Found

  - url: https://www.flylax.com/lax-comments-and-contact-us/lost-and-found

  - source: Los Angeles International Airport

  - domain: flylax.com


  ## Content


  # LAX Lost & Found


  The LAX Lost and Found is managed by the Airport Police Division. Its purpose is
  to reunite people with items left in public areas of Los Angeles International Airport.


  The unit receives property found in areas such as:


  - ticketing areas;

  - baggage claim;

  - gate areas;

  - restaurants and stores;

  - curbside areas;

  - airport parking lots;

  - the LAX-it shuttle and LAX-it lot.


  The Airport Police Lost and Found does not manage or collect items left in Transportation
  Security Administration screening areas or on aircraft. For an item left at a TSA
  checkpoint, use the TSA lost-and-found channel. For an item left on an aircraft,
  contact the airline that operated the flight.


  ## Inquiring About an Item


  The inquiry process is online. A person searching for an item must submit a lost-property
  claim through the LAX Lost and Found process. Submitting a claim is free. A lost-and-found
  claim is an inquiry and is not a police report.


  The information requested in the claim helps the team identify the owner and return
  the item to the correct person. Ownership verification may be required before property
  is released.


  LAX Airport Police primarily communicates about claims by email and may also call
  when more information is needed. Claimants should confirm that they received a submission
  confirmation and should check spam or junk folders for follow-up messages.


  Claims are reviewed on business days, excluding holidays. Response time varies,
  and the page advises that review commonly takes several business days.


  ## Third-Party Services and Software Providers


  LAX Airport Police does not charge a service fee to submit an inquiry and is not
  affiliated with general third-party companies or websites that offer to search for
  lost property.


  The LAX team does use software to inventory found property and review inquiries.
  Messages from the official process may therefore use the software-provider email
  domains `@crowdfind.com` or `@pixithq.com`. Pixit Powered by Crowdfind supplies
  the inventory and inquiry software, but it does not physically operate the LAX Lost
  and Found and should not be contacted for claim updates.



  ## Other Lost-and-Found Responsibilities


  Different organizations handle property depending on where it was left:


  - TSA handles items associated with its screening areas.

  - Airlines handle suitcases and personal items left on aircraft.

  - Dining, shopping, transportation, shuttle, parking, and customs-related losses
  may have separate contacts.

  - Lost passports may require a separate customs-related contact.


  If an item is matched, the claimant will receive recovery instructions. Property
  may be returned in person or shipped at the claimant''s expense. The inquiry itself
  remains free; shipping costs are separate from submitting a lost-item claim.


  LAX Airport Police retains found property for a limited period. Unclaimed property
  is disposed of according to applicable law.

  '

title: LAX Lost & Found
url: https://www.flylax.com/lax-comments-and-contact-us/lost-and-found

- **Flight:** DL417, JFK → LAX
- **Booking reference:** DL7K2M
- **Custody record:** DL-LF-LAX-88421
- **Item reference:** LP-301

The provisional LAX airport report was **not submitted**, and no passport number, identity-document image, or payment information was included.
